lore.name: Kahaan
lore.tagline: A prince bound by duty, driven by truth
lore.description: Born into the military elite, Kahaan views the world through his floating HUD monocle—a distinct electric-blue tick over his left eye. He carries the state's authority in the neural mag-holsters at his ribs. When assigned the [REDACTED] case, he expects a procedural cleanup. Instead, he finds a thread that threatens to unravel the Mesh itself.
lore.quote: Truth is just data waiting to be verified.

lore.name: Rudra
lore.tagline: Disciplined warrior. Grey Trident legacy.
lore.description: The Mountain Shadow. Rudra wields the Old Trident—battered iron, heavier than sin, and utterly unlike the [REDACTED] symbols of the new terrorists. A pacifist by doctrine, he carries the silence of the Himalayas into the noise of the city. He fights not to destroy, but to correct.
lore.quote: The mountain does not hate the wind. It simply endures.

lore.name: General Pratap
lore.tagline: Order requires sacrifice. He decides who pays.
lore.description: The Smiling Architect of Order. With a regulation-neat chevron moustache and the Director's Signet on his right hand, Pratap doesn't just enforce the Mesh—he designed it. He views the [REDACTED] bombings not as a tragedy, but as an audit. Order requires sacrifice, and he has the ledger.
lore.quote: Chaos is a choice. Order is a responsibility.

lore.name: Hana
lore.tagline: The East remembers what the Mesh forgot
lore.description: The Moral Lens. Left-handed and sharp as her chin-length wedge bob, Hana bridges the gap between Indrapur's glass towers and the silent, snowy East. She knows the treaty zones better than the map-makers, and she knows that not all truth survives the upload.

lore.name: Arshi
lore.tagline: Some presences are felt before they're understood
lore.description: The Hidden Heir. Marked by the deep blue eyes of the Akakpen bloodline, she moves through the city like a glitch in the render. She carries a white-ceramic pulse gun and the weight of a broken treaty. Some presences are felt before they are understood.

lore.name: Indrapur HQ
lore.tagline: The nerve center of absolute authority
lore.description: The Oval Fortress. Inside, the Elliptical Glass Map-Table glows with real-time data, watched over by the Tri-Anchor Wall. Here, in the cold light of the command panes, the silence is the ultimate luxury. Access is absolute; shadows are forbidden.

lore.name: Lakshmanpur
lore.tagline: Industrial heartland where smoke meets surveillance
lore.description: Northern river-plain metropole defined by fog-prone winters and dense surveillance infrastructure. The industrial powerhouse of Bharatvarsh features foundries, manufacturing plants, and research facilities. The Mesh infrastructure here is exceptionally dense, monitoring both production quotas and worker movements. OxyPole towers stand at 60-80m intervals, scrubbing air and mounting hovercams. Efficiency defines daily life in this city of pale limestone and vertical gardens.

lore.name: Akakpen Tribe
lore.tagline: Where ancient ways resist modern control
lore.description: Semi-autonomous treaty people maintaining governance through the Queen's Council in the Eastern and Himalayan wilds. Operating under non-lethal first doctrine for tribe-versus-tribe disputes, lethal force reserved only for outside invaders. The Akakpen preserve traditions from before the Mesh, holding territories in no-fly valleys like Silent Throat and Knife-Wind Chute. Their autonomy represents a crack in the regime's absolute control—a reminder that alternatives exist.

lore.name: Bharatsena
lore.tagline: Polite inevitability. Order through control.
lore.description: The ruling military directorate that has governed Bharatvarsh since the Year-Turn Decree of 1985. Operating through 'polite inevitability'—the governance philosophy that makes compliance feel natural. The Bharatsena maintains the surveillance state, controls the Mesh, and has delivered four decades of stability at the cost of civil liberties. Wrist-scans, hovercams, and checkpoint algorithms ensure total visibility. Curfew runs 21:00 to 06:00 in all metropoles.

lore.name: Bracecomm
lore.tagline: Command at your wrist. The Mesh in your sleeve.
lore.description: Sleeve-tile interface standard for military command communication. Integrated biometric authentication, secure encrypted channels, and real-time tactical data display. The device that makes the Mesh personal—every officer's lifeline to the command structure. Tile displays in cyan/blue-white at 30-50% opacity, auto-dismissing on interruption. Silent band operation ensures discretion in sensitive environments.
lore.specifications: Under-fabric sleeve nodes | Tile sizes: 80×160mm (call), 120×240mm (map) | Biometric-locked | Encrypted comm channels

lore.name: Dart
lore.tagline: Silent. Precise. Non-lethal by design.
lore.description: Low-draw ballista system utilizing sleep-sap ammunition. Standard issue for Akakpen treaty enforcement, designed for incapacitation rather than lethality. The weapon of choice when the goal is capture, not kill. Mounted in observation rings at 60-90m perimeters, these devices embody the non-lethal first doctrine that governs tribal conflict resolution.
lore.specifications: 10-15m effective range | Sleep-sap payload | Red cap safety trigger | Non-lethal designation

lore.name: Oxy Pole
lore.tagline: They scrub the air. They see everything.
lore.description: Air-scrub towers standing 8.5-9.2 meters tall, spaced at 60-80 meter intervals throughout urban zones. OxyPoles serve dual purpose: atmospheric purification and surveillance platform mounting. Graphite powder-coated aluminum masts support crown vanes and wash heads. Hovercams dock at their peaks. The infrastructure that makes the Mesh possible—and inescapable.
lore.specifications: 8.5-9.2m height | 60-80m spacing | Air purification + surveillance mounts | Tri-mount: PA node, camera nest, environmental sensor

lore.name: Pulse Gun
lore.tagline: Air becomes wall. Crowds become controlled.
lore.description: Air-curtain dispersal tool for crowd control operations. Generates targeted pressure waves to direct or disperse civilian gatherings without kinetic projectiles. Standard equipment for Mesh enforcement units. Part of the permitted non-lethal device set, deployed alongside fine water mist and 48-hour expirable dye markers. The tool of polite inevitability made physical.
lore.specifications: Air-curtain technology | Non-projectile crowd control | Variable intensity | Directional sound sweep (max 88 dB)

lore.name: Surya
lore.tagline: He trusts nothing connected to the system
lore.description: The Ghost Operative. You won't find Surya in any active service file. His Brace-Comm runs dark—permanently—because he learned the hard way that everything connected can be turned against you. After the Guhyakas unit erased his service record and buried the men he served with, Surya vanished into the signal gaps of Bharatvarsh. Now he moves through the country like interference on a clean feed: matte graphite jacket, hands steady at his belt, collecting evidence with the patience of a man who knows that one photograph in daylight is worth more than a hundred bullets in the dark.
lore.quote: Evidence doesn't need a rank. It just needs daylight.

hotspots.label: DARK BRACE-COMM
hotspots.description: Display permanently OFF — trust nothing connected

lore.name: Kaali
lore.tagline: Her word is binding covenant for an entire people
lore.description: The Wounded Queen. They brought her to the ICU on a stretcher but they could not take the throne from her voice. Kaali has led the Akakpen for decades—not from a palace, but from council fires and treaty tables, from rain-soaked mountain crossings and the quiet work of holding a multi-faith community together by sheer force of listening. Now she lies bandaged and breathing through a mask, her gold hair-ring placed carefully on the bedside tray. One emerald-green eye does all the work. When she whispers a promise to the old comrade she hasn't seen in years, the fog in the room shifts. Nations will move because of what she says next.
lore.quote: A queen does not command the mountain. She listens until the mountain speaks.

hotspots.label: EMERALD COMMAND EYES
hotspots.description: The 'command line' — green eyes, never blue

hotspots.label: GOLD HAIR-RING
hotspots.description: Antique gold, satin finish — authority talisman

lore.name: The Treaty Zone
lore.tagline: Where the Mesh ends and the mountains remember
lore.description: The Mesh ends at the treeline. Beyond it, the Treaty Zone begins—a mountainous band where state troops halt at checkpoints and wait for tribal consent that may never come. In valleys called Silent Throat and Knife-Wind Chute, drone-jammers run permanently and radar screens show nothing but weather. The architecture here is wood and stone and slate, hand-mended and honest. Prayer flags hang stiff at altitude because the wind at four thousand metres doesn't flutter—it shears. Fog shafts through pine canopy. Frost glitters on rope fibres strung between sentinel trees. This is not a rebellion. It is a proof of concept: that a world without the Mesh is not only possible, but already forty years old.

lore.name: Mysuru
lore.tagline: Plateau tech-hub where software meets surveillance
lore.description: Mysuru sits on a plateau where the sun hits harder and the secrets run deeper. Peninsular Command's crown jewel is a city of basalt and dark glass, where the brightest engineers in Bharatvarsh build software and optics they will not be permitted to discuss for five to fifteen years. OxyPole crowns here carry dust-mesh louvers against the red laterite haze, and the UV-resistant coatings on every surface give the architecture a faintly iridescent sheen. The campus culture is civilian in dress code, military in clearance level. Breakthroughs happen behind biometric doors. Papers are written, classified, and locked before the ink dries.

lore.name: Tribhuj
lore.tagline: Democracy died in 1984. Its ghost carries a trident.
lore.description: Before 1984, the trident was a ballot paper. Tribhuj began as a civilian movement for elected governance—peaceful, principled, and inconvenient enough to be declared treasonous alongside religion, caste, and surnames in one sweeping decree. After the ban, the symbols didn't die. They went to the walls. Grey tridents scratched into alley plaster at 3 a.m. Pamphlets folded into chapati wrappers during Festival Fortnight. Basement meetings where the word 'election' was spoken like a prayer. Then, in October 2025, seven cities burned under a flag bearing the same trident. Whether the old movement and the new violence share anything beyond a symbol is the question that will tear Bharatvarsh apart.
lore.quote: The trident was never a weapon. It was a ballot.

lore.name: The Mesh
lore.tagline: Omnipresent. Ambient. Inescapable.
lore.description: Nobody remembers when they stopped noticing the hovercams. The Mesh arrived in three phases over fifteen years, and by the time Phase III went live in 2015, surveillance had become weather—ambient, total, and unremarkable. Biometric wrist-scanners confirm your identity at every kiosk. Teardrop-shaped checkpoint masts read your gait at intersections. OxyPole sensor arrays cataloguing the air also cataloguing you. Micro-expression algorithms flag hesitation before you've finished the thought that caused it. The Mesh is not cruel. It is not loud. It is polite, inevitable, and everywhere. Forty years of perfect stability. The invoice is still being calculated.
lore.specifications: Phase I (2000-05): Biometric ID + checkpoints | Phase II (2006-10): Hovercam grid + Brace-Comm | Phase III (2011-15): Micro-expression AI + predictive routing

lore.name: Neural Diodes
lore.tagline: Where the soldier ends and the weapon begins
lore.description: The scar behind Kahaan's right ear is small enough to miss. What it connects to is not. The neural-diode lattice lets him float twin pistols at shoulder height on faint electric-blue shimmer threads, pilot a scout motorcycle with a thought, and stream tactical data directly to his visual cortex. It is, by every military metric, a masterpiece. It is also slowly breaking him. EMPs knock out everything—weapons, vehicle, HUD—leaving him human and exposed mid-combat. Migraines arrive without warning. His nose bleeds during briefings. And in the quiet hours, he wonders whether the machine is a tool he uses or a system that is using him.
lore.specifications: Surgical diode scallop (right ear) | Mag-holster range: 2m | EMP-vulnerable | Side effects: migraines, tinnitus, transient paralysis

hotspots.label: DIODE SCALLOP
hotspots.description: Faint surgical scar behind right ear

hotspots.label: SHIMMER THREADS
hotspots.description: Electric-blue magnetic link to floating weapons

lore.name: HUD Monocle
lore.tagline: The blue tick that sees what flesh cannot
lore.description: It floats over the left eye like a thought made visible—a curved rectangular lens with no visible apparatus, projecting tiny electric-blue arcs and ticks that scroll too fast to read. The glyphs are deliberately illegible to onlookers; what they display is classified at the point of rendering. When the HUD activates, a soft blue wash spreads across the cheekbone and the surgical scar behind the ear glows faint cyan. In headquarters, the monocle stays dark—officers are expected to think with unassisted eyes. In the field, its activation is a signal: the human layer has invited the machine layer in, and the line between the two has begun to blur.
lore.specifications: Curved rectangular lens | Apparatus-less floating mount | Electric-blue glow | OFF in HQ, ON in field ops
